violations.description: Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Nonfood contact surface * was not cleaned as frequently as required. - Cabinet faces and door handles dirty
Manual cleaning and sanitizing requirements. — --No sanitizer prepped or being used during food operations.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…)* were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. - Floors and walls dirty
General employee cleanliness. — --Food employee did not wash hands as necessary. Hand washing sink was dry and had not been used. Employee not washing hands frequently.

Preventing contamination during storage. — --Food item was not stored in a clean, dry location a minimum of 6 inches above the floor. Boxes of food in the walk in cooler were stored on the floor and not at least 6in up off the floor.

General equipment and utensils - Materials and design. — --Material of nonfood contact surface was not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent and cleanable. Multiple non food contact surfaces are in need of repair. The countertop in the self serve area is cracked and/or laminate seal is broken or missing.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Equipment that dispenses liquid/food/ice in unpackaged form was not cleaned properly and/or as frequently as required. F'real and soda machine require a thorough cleaning.--Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned adequately. Multiple areas in the self serve and food prep area are in need of a thorough cleaning. Equipment is not cleaned adequately and clean in place equipment is not moved to clean in and around. Walk-in coolers have a distinct smell of mold/mildew and have signs of bacterial growth and a build of dust and dirt.

Manual cleaning and sanitizing requirements. — --Sanitizer solution for three compartment sink was not maintained at the proper concentration, There was no sanitizer solution mixed up in the 3 compartment sink. There was no sani-buckets with a sanitizer solution mixed up during hours of operations.--Three compartment sink was not maintained or operated in the approved three step method. 3 Compartment sink is not being utilized properly using the wash rinse and sanitize method. Dirty dishes were put into all 3 compartments.
Handwashing lavatories. — --Handwashing lavatory was used for purpose other than handwashing. handwashing sink had drink can in it. Sink was dirty and not used.

Toilet facilities. — --Toilet room used by women did not contain at least one covered waste receptacle. Receptacle in women's room did not have a cover on it. Trash had not been taken out in women's room. Men's room closed for maintenance.

Design and construction of floors, walls, and ceilings. — --Floors, walls, and/or ceiling were not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent, and easily cleanable. Floors walls and ceiling throughout the facility are in need of repair. Ceiling tiles have water stains and are cracked and broken. Floor tiles are cracked and or missing. Walls need to be repaired and holes sealed.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…) were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Ceiling tiles have water stains and a build up of dirt and dust. Floors and walls need a serious thorough cleaning throughout the store.

Storage and handling of maintenance items. — --Wet-use maintenance items, such as mops, were not placed in a position that allows proper air-drying. Mop and bucket not being used properly. Wet mop is being left in dirty mop water. Mop bucket should be dumped when not in use. and turned upside down. Mop should be rung out and hung to drip and dry.
